title: Full genome PCR amplification of all African Hepatitis B Virus genotypes

steps: Prepare the following two mixes on ice:

Master Mix 1: For one 15 µL reaction combine the following: A B C D 1 dNTPs 25 mM 200 uM 0.2 uL 2 P1 15 uM 300 nM 0.5 uL 3 P2 or P2_A1 15 uM 300 nM 0.5 uL 4 Expand Hi Fi Buffer 10x 1x 2 uL 5 MgCl2 15 mM 1.5 mM - 6 ddH2O n.a. n.a. 11.8 uL Components for Master Mix 1 with their stock and final concentrations and volume needed for one 15 uL reaction.

Master Mix 2: For one 5 µL reaction combine the following: A B C D 1 Expand Hi Fi Buffer 10x 1x 0.5 uL 2 Expand Hi Fi Enzyme 3.5 U/uL 2.6 U 0.75 uL 3 ddH2O n.a. n.a. 3.75 uL Components for Master Mix 2 with their stock and final concentrations and volume needed for one 5 uL reaction.
Add 15 µL, Master Mix 1 to 5 µL, template DNA for a 20 µL reaction in thin walled PCR tubes. At the Thermal cycler, add an additional 5 µL, Master Mix 2 to each tube after initial denaturation.

Perform thermal cycling according to the following conditions: Initial denaturation at 94 °C for 2 minutes. Cooling to 58 °C before adding Mix 2. 40 cycles of: Denaturation at 94 °C for 40 seconds Annealing at 55 °C for 90 seconds Elongation at 68 °C for 180 seconds* *Add 180 seconds every 10 cycles Cooling/hold at 4 °C
Perform TBE-gel electrophoresis to confirm success of amplification prior to amplicon clean-up.
creator: Louis-Stéphane Le Clercq
